extraction.one_line_summary: TERPSTRA Niki attacked solo on the final cobbled climb with 15km to go, held off PEDERSEN Mads and GILBERT Philippe to give Quick-Step their 21st win of the early season.
tactical_patterns: TERPSTRA Niki attacked directly after Quick-Step teammates exhausted chasers, exploiting positioned fatigue rather than raw power differential.
PEDERSEN Mads emerged as surprise runner-up despite no lead-out, signaling new Classics contender status at 22 years old.
VANMARCKE Sep crashed early race, BMC's pre-race favorite immediately compromised before selective climbs began.
Quick-Step controlled entire race despite world's teams combining against them, 21 wins proving numbers trump coalition tactics.

rider_signals.TERPSTRA Niki: positive: Perfectly timed solo attack after teammates absorbed chasing duties, first Dutch winner in 32 years demonstrates tactical patience.
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: Second place from nowhere at 22 suggests breakthrough Classics capability, moved to contender status without team support.
rider_signals.VANMARCKE Sep: negative: Pre-race favorite crashed early, BMC's protected rider compromised before race selection began.
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: positive: Third place podium finish shows enduring Classics strength despite Quick-Step team dominance.
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: neutral: Fourth place finish respectable but unable to counter Quick-Step numerical advantage.
rider_signals.BENOOT Tiesj: neutral: Top-10 finish for young rider suggests developing Classics engine.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Placed in group but never in winning position, Quick-Step tactics neutralized individual strength.
rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: neutral: Finished in main group, unable to follow decisive Terpstra move despite positioning.
rider_signals.NAESEN Oliver: neutral: Mentioned in early crash involving multiple riders, race compromised from start.
